data.title: Consumer Cos Up After Inflation Data -- Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rallied following muted inflation data.

The Consumer Price Index rose 0.3% in September, which was cooler than the 0.4% rate economists had projected. The data indicate less pressure on U.S. shoppers ahead of the key holiday shopping season, and also open the door for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates.

The University of Michigan's consumer-sentiment index for October fell to 53.6 from an initial October reading of 55 published two weeks ago and 55.1 last month.

Alaska Air Group said that more than 360 flights had been canceled after a technology outage disrupted its operations for hours late Thursday, prompting the carrier to delay its earnings call.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1747ET

data.title: Health Care Up After HCA, Sanofi Earnings -- Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies ticked up after a generally strong round of earnings.

Hospital chain HCA Healthcare boosted its full-year growth outlook after logging higher profit and revenue in the third quarter, as same-facility admissions grew.

French drug maker Sanofi shares rose after it said a jump in sales of its blockbuster Dupixent anti-inflammatory drug and robust pharmaceutical launches drove higher revenue and adjusted earnings for the third quarter. Sanofi said it remained in talks with the U.S. government over drug pricing.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1747ET

data.title: Industrials Up on Optimism About Inflation Trends, Trump -- Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies ticked up as optimism about inflation trends and Federal Reserve policy was offset by tariff concerns.

There were some indications of tariff pressures in the report, according to one brokerage. While non-vehicle goods prices registered their second-strongest print year to date, vehicle prices remained subdued.

German luxury car maker Porsche posted its first quarterly loss in years after taking a hefty charge related to its electric-vehicle products.

Booz Allen slashed its outlook for the year and trimmed its workforce further as the government contractor contends with the shutdown.

General Dynamics shares rose after the defense contractor posted higher third-quarter revenue and profit, driven by higher demand for corporate jets.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1746ET

data.title: Materials Down as Traders Weigh Inflation Data, U.S.-China Tensions -- Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials ticked down after muted inflation data and doubts about the outlook for U.S.-China relations.

Gold futures slipped, losing 1.7% on a volatile week that included major rallies and its biggest one-day drop on record.

Copper futures added modestly to the gains sparked by Thursday's report that President Trump would meet Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping at the APEC meeting in South Korea next week.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1745ET

data.title: Energy Down as Oil Rally Pauses -- Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies fell, giving back some recent gains associated with new U.S. sanctions on Russian oil.

Oil futures slipped for the session, but rose 7.6% to $61.50 for the week due to the plans to impose sanctions on Russian energy majors Lukoil and Rosneft.

In another escalation of tensions between the U.S. and a major oil producer, the Pentagon said it was sending the Navy's most advanced aircraft carrier to the Caribbean in a major escalation of the Trump administration's military campaign to target drug smugglers and threaten Nicolas Maduro's government in Venezuela.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1745ET

data.title: Microsoft's Xbox to Remake Original Halo Video Game
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan


Microsoft's Xbox is remaking the original Halo video game with plans to support multiplayer gaming across several consoles for the first time.

The remake, titled Halo: Campaign Evolved, will support the game's original two-player split screen experience on console as well as allow up to four players across Xbox, PC and PlayStation 5, according to a blog post on Xbox's website.

The game will include new visuals and story content, remastered music and re-recorded voice lines. It will also bring back some weapons and vehicles from later Halo titles, Xbox said.


Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1744ET

NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Team Joins and Connects the Lockheed Martin Developed Orion Spacecraft with the Space Launch System Rocket for the Upcoming Artemis II Mission
data.description:
In a move signifying mission readiness, NASA's Exploration Ground Systems (EGS) team recently joined and connected the Lockheed Martin developed Orion spacecraft with the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket for the upcoming Artemis II mission. The Orion spacecraft, named Integrity by its astronaut crew, was transported to the Vehicle Assembly Building at the Kennedy Space Center where it was lifted atop the SLS rocket in High Bay 3 on Oct. 19.

This marks a major milestone for the Artemis II mission as NASA and industry work toward a historic launch to the Moon with four astronauts early next year. Following the mating, teams will make electrical and data connections between Orion and SLS, along with umbilical connections from the mobile launch platform to the spacecraft. EGS will conduct integrated tests of Orion and SLS before they are rolled out to Launch Pad 39B for a wet dress rehearsal next year.

The first crewed flight of the Artemis program is set to launch no earlier than February 2026, with potential launch windows extending through April 2026. The mission will be 10 days and is a key event to establishing a long-term presence at the Moon for exploration and science.

data.title: Elanco Animal Health Obtains FDA Authorization for Screwworm Treatment
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton


Elanco Animal Health received an emergency-use authorization from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its tablets for treating screwworm.

The FDA said Friday it had given the authorization after concluding that it is reasonable to believe that the tablets, called Credelio, may be effective in treating New World screwworm infestations in dogs and puppies.

This is the first time the FDA has issued an emergency-use authorization for an animal drug, the agency said. In August, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., determined that New World screwworm presents significant potential for a public health emergency. He gave the FDA the ability to issue emergency-use authorizations for animal drugs to prevent screwworm.

Most dogs in the U.S. are at low risk of screwworm, but dogs near the U.S.-Mexico border that have travelled to countries with active cases are more likely to be exposed. The parasite poses a threat to livestock and food security, the FDA said.


Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1724ET

IBM says conventional AMD chips can run quantum computing error correction algorithm
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) -IBM said on Friday it can run a key quantum computing error correction algorithm on commonly available chips from Advanced Micro Devices, a step toward commercializing super-powerful computers.

The U.S. stalwart is racing to develop quantum computing against Microsoft and Alphabet's Google, which announced a breakthrough algorithm this week.

Quantum computers use what are known as qubits to tackle problems that would take conventional computers thousands of years to crack - problems such as how trillions of atoms react over time. However, qubits are prone to errors that can quickly overwhelm the useful computing work of a quantum chip.

In June, IBM said it had developed an algorithm to run alongside quantum chips that can address such errors. In a research paper seen by Reuters to be published on Monday, IBM will show it can run those algorithms in real time on a type of chip called a field programmable gate array manufactured by AMD.

Jay Gambetta, director of IBM research, said the work showed that IBM's algorithm not only works in the real world, but can operate on a readily available AMD chip that is not "ridiculously expensive." 

"Implementing it, and showing that the implementation is actually 10 times faster than what is needed, is a big deal," Gambetta said in an interview.

IBM has a multi-year plan to build a quantum computer called Starling by 2029. Gambetta said the algorithm work disclosed Friday was completed a year ahead of schedule.

IBM shares closed up 7.88% at $397.46 on Friday after the news, and AMD shares closed up 7.63% at $252.92.

(Reporting by Stephen Nellis in San Francisco; Editing by Christopher Cushing and David Gregorio)

By Stephen Nellis

PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported net income was IDR 15,115,133 million compared to IDR 16,308,385 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was IDR 405 compared to IDR 437 a year ago.

Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was IDR 405 compared to IDR 437 a year ago.
